chapters.title: Work With Constraints Instead of Complaining About Them

summaryBullets: Treating constraints as decision facts that shape what is possible creates clearer judgment.
Better strategy begins when people stop fighting the limits they cannot wish away.
This matters because friction and tradeoffs become easier to manage when the real constraint is visible.
A common mistake is spending energy on goals that ignore physical, financial, or time limits.
The pattern becomes easier to see when people keep pushing harder but nothing changes because the bottleneck stayed untouched.
A practical starting move is to name the binding constraint, reduce the wasted motion around it, and redesign the plan to fit reality.
keyTakeaways: Treating constraints as decision facts that shape what is possible creates clearer judgment. This perspective helps people make clearer decisions and follow through with more discipline when stakes rise.
